codeReview.summary: Solid implementation covering all expected behaviors. The text editor correctly tracks shift key state and selection anchor, extends/contracts selection via Shift+Left/Right, clears on regular arrows/Home/End, replaces on character input, and deletes on backspace. The compositor receives selection range via MSG_SELECTION_UPDATE (type 33) and renders a semi-transparent blue highlight behind selected characters using the new draw_tt_sel() method. MSG_WRITE_DELETE_RANGE (type 34) handles bulk deletion with correct bounds checking. The IPC message ordering is correct — the editor sends multiple messages atomically (before signaling), and the compositor drains them all in one event loop iteration. Selection range normalization handles reversed anchor/cursor correctly. Cursor bar is correctly suppressed when selection is active. draw_tt delegates to draw_tt_sel with no-selection defaults, maintaining backward compatibility. 9 well-targeted tests covering highlight rendering, range normalization, byte-to-xy mapping, editing operations, color contrast, and cursor suppression.

issues.description: Module-level docstring still lists only MSG_WRITE_INSERT and MSG_WRITE_DELETE as sent messages. Should include MSG_SELECTION_UPDATE (33), MSG_WRITE_DELETE_RANGE (34), and MSG_CURSOR_MOVE (32) for completeness.

The cursor_bar_suppressed_with_selection test only verifies the boolean logic (s_lo < s_hi), not that draw_tt_sel actually suppresses cursor pixels. A pixel-based test (render with selection, check cursor position has no cursor-colored pixels) would be more robust against regressions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: architecture.md does not document the IPC message types used between the editor and compositor. It mentions MSG_KEY_EVENT and MSG_PRESENT but omits the editor protocol messages (MSG_WRITE_INSERT=30, MSG_WRITE_DELETE=31, MSG_CURSOR_MOVE=32, MSG_SELECTION_UPDATE=33, MSG_WRITE_DELETE_RANGE=34). As the editor protocol grows, this gap will make it harder for future workers to understand the IPC surface.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: architecture.md lines 16-19 mention only MSG_KEY_EVENT and MSG_PRESENT. The full editor protocol (5 message types) is only documented in source code constants.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used inline Color::rgb()/rgba() constants in compositor's render_content_surface rather than palette constants. This was consistent with the pre-existing code at the time, and the later polish commit (2545c2e) centralized all colors to palette.rs. Not a worker error — the pattern wasn't established yet. But AGENTS.md could mention preferring palette constants for new color usage.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit 3218d03 compositor diff: Color::rgba(50, 80, 160, 180) inline. Later commit 2545c2e moved to drawing::TEXT_SELECTION.

summary: Text selection implementation passes review. All 9 expected behaviors from the feature description are correctly implemented across 4 files (drawing library, compositor, text editor, tests). The IPC protocol extension (2 new message types) is clean and follows the existing pattern. Selection state management in the editor is correct with proper shift key tracking. The compositor's rendering path correctly delegates to draw_tt_sel with highlight and cursor suppression. Edge cases (empty selection collapse, full-document selection, boundary conditions) are handled. Two non-blocking issues: stale module docstring and a boolean-only cursor suppression test. Two shared state observations about architecture.md IPC documentation gap and color constant conventions.
